title: Kinetic Theory of Gases Test - 3 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: The volume V of an enclosure contains a mixture of three gases, 16 g of oxygen, 28 g of nitrogen and 44 g of carbon dioxide at absolute temperature T. Consider R as universal gas constant. The pressure of the mixture of gases is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
No. of moles of O2 :
n1 = $${{16} \over {32}}$$ = 0.5 mole

No. of moles of N2 :
n2 = $${{28} \over {28}}$$ = 1 mole

No. of moles of CO2 :
n3 = $${{44} \over {44}}$$ = 1 mole

Total no. of moles in container : n = n1 + n2 + n3

$$ \therefore $$ n = 0.5 + 1 + 1 = $${5 \over 2}$$ moles

Now; PV = nRT

$$ \Rightarrow $$ P = $${{nRT} \over V}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ P = $${5 \over 2}{{RT} \over V}$$

questions.questionText: 300 cal. of heat is given to a heat engine and it rejects 225 cal. If source temperature is 227$$^\circ$$C, then the temperature of sink will be ______________ $$^\circ$$C.

of fusion of ice is 80 cal/g] the value of M is ____
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A flask contains argon and oxygen in the ratio of 3 : 2 in mass and the mixture is kept at 27$$^\circ$$C. The ratio of their average kinetic energy per molecule respectively will be :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$K{E_{avg}} = {3 \over 2}kT$$ (At lower temperature)

As temperature is same for both the gases.

$$\Rightarrow$$ Both gases will have same average kinetic energy.

$$ \Rightarrow {{{{(K{E_{avg}})}_{\arg on}}} \over {{{(K{E_{avg}})}_{oxygen}}}} = {1 \over 1}$$

questions.questionText: Heat is supplied to a diatomic gas at constant pressure. Then the ratio of $\Delta Q : \Delta U : \Delta W$ is ______.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
For a diatomic gas (assuming no vibrational modes), the degrees of freedom $f = 5$.

The molar specific heat at constant volume for diatomic gas is

$$C_v = \frac{f}{2}R = \frac{5}{2}R$$

The molar specific heat at constant pressure is

$$C_p = C_v + R = \frac{5}{2}R + R = \frac{7}{2}R$$

The heat supplied at constant pressure is

$$\Delta Q = nC_p\Delta T = \frac{7}{2}nR\Delta T$$

The change in internal energy is

$$\Delta U = nC_v\Delta T = \frac{5}{2}nR\Delta T$$

The work done at constant pressure is

$$\Delta W = P\Delta V$$

From ideal gas equation,

$$PV = nRT \rightarrow P\Delta V = nR\Delta T$$

So, the work done at constant pressure is

$$\Delta W = P\Delta V = nR\Delta T = \frac{2}{2}nR\Delta T$$

So, the ratio $\Delta Q : \Delta U : \Delta W$ is:

$$\frac{7}{2} : \frac{5}{2} : \frac{2}{2} \rightarrow 7:5:2$$

Therefore, the correct option is (D).

questions.questionText: The temperature at which the kinetic energy of oxygen molecules becomes double than its value at $$27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
The kinetic energy of an ideal gas is given by the equation:

$KE = \frac{3}{2} kT$

where (k) is Boltzmann's constant and (T) is the absolute temperature in kelvins. Therefore, the kinetic energy of a gas is directly proportional to its temperature.

If the kinetic energy doubles, the temperature must also double. The original temperature is given as ($27^\circ C$), which is equal to (300 K) in absolute terms. Therefore, the final temperature ($T_f$) in kelvins is:

$T_f = 2 \cdot 300 K = 600 K$

Converting this back to degrees Celsius gives:

$T_f = 600K - 273 = 327 ^\circ C$

questions.questionText: A thermodynamic system is taken through cyclic process. The total work done in the process is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The temperature of equal masses of three different liquids x, y and z are 10$$^\circ$$C, 20$$^\circ$$C and 30$$^\circ$$C respectively. The temperature of mixture when x is mixed with y is 16$$^\circ$$C and that when y is mixed with z is 26$$^\circ$$C. The temperature of mixture when x and z are mixed will be :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
when x and y are mixed, Tf1 = 16$$^\circ$$C

m1s1T + m2s2T2 = (m1s1 + m2s2)Tf1

s1 $$\times$$ 10 + s2 $$\times$$ 20 = (s1 + s2) $$\times$$ 16

s1 = $${2 \over 3}$$s2 .... (i)

when y and z are mixed, Tf2 = 26$$^\circ$$C

m2s2T + m3s3T3 = (m3s3 + m3s3)Tf2

s2 $$\times$$ 20 + s3 $$\times$$ 30 = (s2 + s3) $$\times$$ 26

s3 = $${3 \over 2}$$s2 ..... (ii)

when x and z are mixed

m1s1T1 + m3s3T3 = (m1s1 + m3s3)Tf

$${2 \over 3}$$s2 $$\times$$ 10 + $${2 \over 3}$$s2 $$\times$$ 20 = $$\left( {{2 \over 3}{s_2} + {3 \over 2}{s_2}} \right){T_f}$$

Tf = 23.84$$^\circ$$C

questions.questionText: An ideal gas has undergone through the cyclic process as shown in the figure. Work done by the gas in the entire cycle is ________ $\times 10^{-1} \mathrm{~J}$. (Take $\pi=3.14$ )
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution: $$\begin{aligned} &\text { Area of circle, } W=\frac{\pi}{4} d_1 d_2\\ &\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{W}=\frac{\pi}{4}(500-300) \times 10^3(350-150) \times 10^{-6} \\ & \mathrm{~W}=31.4 \mathrm{Joule} \\ & \mathrm{~W}=314 \times 10^{-1} \mathrm{Joule} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

questions.questionText: The heat absorbed by a system in going through the given cyclic process is :
questions.subject: Physics
